info.title: F5 Distributed Cloud Web App Scanning - Public API
info.description:
Public API for customers using API key authentication.

**Authentication:** A valid API key must be included in the `Authorization` header—e.g., `Authorization: WAS vPhAHspjbWMXaiyJfAXjFHAEzpRQaYuG` or `Authorization: Heyhack vPhAHspjbWMXaiyJfAXjFHAEzpRQaYuG`. You can create an API key on [the Settings page](https://app.heyhack.com/settings).

contact.name: F5 Distributed Cloud Web App Scanning Support

get.summary: Internal web use only. Returns the application name.

post.summary: Internal web use only. Creates a new user account.

requestBody.description: The account object containing the user's information.

post.summary: Internal web use only. Creates a new user account directly without the need for an invitation.

requestBody.description: The direct account object containing the user's information.

post.summary: Internal use only. This API lets users log into Heyhack directly from the F5 Distributed Cloud console.

requestBody.description: An object containing the tenant ID, user ID, and user email address.

get.summary: Internal web use only. Exports an overview of all completed penetration tests using the Scan service in Web App Scanning.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format of the exported services. Can be "json" or "csv". Default is "json".

post.summary: Creates a new application and starts a penetration test.

requestBody.description: An object containing the application's information.

post.summary: Scanner use only. Register a new finding for a specific test report.

requestBody.description: The finding object formatted as JSON.

get.summary: Retrieves all vulnerabilities found by Heyhack. You can choose to filter the vulnerabilities by the application they are related to, whether they're accepted, and their CVSS score.

parameters.name: application_id
parameters.description: Optional. Provide the ID of the application to filter vulnerabilities.

post.summary: Scanner use only. Register a new API endpoint for a specific test report.

requestBody.description: The finding object formatted as JSON.

get.summary: Internal web use only. Retrieves all vulnerabilities in a format compatible with BIG-IP Application Security Manager.

parameters.name: application_id
parameters.description: Optional. Provide the ID of the application to filter vulnerabilities.

parameters.name: test_report_id
parameters.description: Optional. Provide the ID of the test report to filter vulnerabilities.

get.summary: Scanner use only. Fetches one Recon job from the queue.

post.summary: Starts a Recon job.

requestBody.description: An object containing the Recon job's information.

patch.summary: Scanner use only. Completes a Recon job.

requestBody.description: Object containing information on the finished Recon job.

post.summary: Scanner use only. Creates or updates a domain found by Recon.

requestBody.description: Body of the found domain.

post.summary: Scanner use only. Creates or updates a service found by Recon.

requestBody.description: Body of the found service.

get.summary: Internal web use only. Exports all services of all Recon jobs associated with a specific customer.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format of the exported services. Can be "json" or "csv". Default is "json".

post.summary: Scanner use only. Creates or updates a finding for a service found by Recon.

requestBody.description: Body of the finding.

get.summary: Internal web use only. Exports all findings of all Recon jobs associated with a specific customer.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format of the exported findings. Can be "json" or "csv". Default is "json".

get.summary: Internal web use only. Exports the services of a specific Recon job.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format of the exported services. Can be "json" or "csv". Default is "json".

get.summary: Internal web use only. Exports the findings of a specific Recon job.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format of the exported findings. Can be "json" or "csv". Default is "json".

get.summary: Checks the status of a Recon job. If it's complete, the results are returned.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format of the exported services. Can be "json" or "csv". Default is "json".

parameters.name: delete
parameters.description: Set to `true` if the Recon job should be deleted after the job has completed and the results are returned.

post.summary: Scanner use only. Calculates Security Scores for all customers.

get.summary: Scanner use only. Fetches one test job from the queue and marks it as started.

post.summary: Starts a new penetration test.
post.description:
You must provide either a test profile ID (to start a penetration test using a specific test profile) or
an application ID (to start a penetration test using the Default Profile of the application).

patch.summary: Scanner use only. Completes a test job.

requestBody.description: A dictionary of vulnerabilities and how many succeeded test cases and failed test cases each vulnerability has. The key of the dictionary must be a key in `vulnerabilities.json` (e.g., `DOM_BASED_XSS`).

get.summary: Scanner use only. Checks if a test report is supposed to keep running.

post.summary: Scanner use only. Provides information on page round-trip times in the scanned application.

requestBody.description: A list of the found pages with round-trip times and other relevant information.

post.summary: Scanner use only. Provides information on login page URLs found during the scan.

requestBody.description: A list of the URLs of the login pages found.

post.summary: Scanner use only. To report if a scan has timed out.
Scanner use only. Provides information on user logins during the scan.

requestBody.description: A dictionary of the users and their status codes.

get.summary: Checks the status of a Scan job. If it's complete, the results are returned and the app is deleted.

parameters.name: wib_format
parameters.description: Set to `true` if the results should be returned in Wib's format.

post.summary: Scanner use only. Uploads a file to storage.

get.summary: Internal web use only. Retrieves a file from storage by key.

Exports a list of actions conducted by users or via API requests in Web App Scanning (using header-based authentication). The total number of available entries matching the filters will be return in the X-Total-Count response header.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: Number of entries to return. Maximum 1,000.

get.summary: Exports an overview of all completed penetration tests using the Scan service in Web App Scanning (using header-based authentication).

post.summary: Creates a new group in the tenant of a specific customer. Returns the ID of the group after it's been created.

requestBody.description: An object containing the desired name of the group.

post.summary: Adds a member to a group.

requestBody.description: An object containing the member's email address and their role within the group (0 = Monitor, 1 = User, 2 = Admin).

get.summary: Retrieves all applications associated with a specific customer.

post.summary: Creates a new application for a specific customer.

requestBody.description: An object containing the application's information.

get.summary: Retrieve the details of a specific application.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the application to retrieve.

delete.summary: Deletes an existing application for a specific customer.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the application to be deleted.

get.summary: Retrieve the test profiles associated with a specific application.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the application to retrieve.

get.summary: Retrieves all test profiles associated with a specific customer.
Retrieves all vulnerabilities found by Heyhack. You can choose to filter the vulnerabilities by the application they are related to.

parameters.name: application_id
parameters.description: Optional. Provide the ID of the application to filter vulnerabilities.

get.summary: Retrieves all API endpoints found by Heyhack. You can choose to filter the API endpoints by the application they are related to.

parameters.name: application_id
parameters.description: Optional. Provide the ID of the application to filter API endpoints.

parameters.name: wib_format
parameters.description: Set to `true` if the results should be returned in Wib's format.

get.summary: Retrieves all vulnerabilities in a format compatible with BIG-IP Application Security Manager. You can choose to filter the vulnerabilities by the application or the test report they are related to.

parameters.name: application_id
parameters.description: Optional. Provide the ID of the application to filter vulnerabilities.

parameters.name: test_report_id
parameters.description: Optional. Provide the ID of the test report to filter vulnerabilities.

get.summary: Retrieves a single issue with detailed vulnerability metadata and the most recent finding.

parameters.name: issue_id
parameters.description: Required. The ID of the issue to retrieve.

get.summary: Retrieves all issues for a specific test report, enriched with vulnerability metadata and the most recent finding for each issue.

parameters.name: report_id
parameters.description: Required. The ID of the test report.

get.summary: Generates and returns a Markdown report for a specific test report. The Markdown is optimized for consumption by AI agents.

parameters.name: report_id
parameters.description: Required. The ID of the test report.

get.summary: Exports all services of all Recon jobs associated with a specific customer (using an API key).

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format of the exported services. Can be "json" or "csv". Default is "json".

get.summary: Exports all findings of all Recon jobs associated with a specific customer (using an API key).

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format of the exported findings. Can be "json" or "csv". Default is "json".

get.summary: Exports the services of a specific Recon job using an API key.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format of the exported services. Can be "json" or "csv". Default is "json".

get.summary: Exports the findings of a specific Recon job using an API key.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format of the exported findings. Can be "json" or "csv". Default is "json".

get.summary: Checks the status of a Scan job.
Generates a link to download the video of a Scan job. The link is valid for 12 hours after generation.
Retrieves the full vulnerability dictionary, including CWE IDs and OWASP Top Ten categories.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Optional. Filter vulnerabilities by name, abbreviation, or CWE ID.

get.summary: Retrieves all schedules for a specific Recon asset.

put.summary: Creates or updates a schedule for a specific Recon asset.
put.description: If the asset already has schedules, they will be replaced.

requestBody.description: The schedule configuration.

delete.summary: Deletes all schedules for a specific Recon asset.

put.summary: Bulk set schedules for multiple Recon assets.
put.description:
The `conflictBehavior` parameter controls what happens when an asset already has a schedule:
- `Replace`: Remove existing schedules and set the new one (default)
- `Add`: Add the new schedule alongside existing ones
- `Fail`: Fail for assets that already have schedules

requestBody.description: The bulk schedule request.

delete.summary: Bulk delete schedules from multiple Recon assets.

requestBody.description: The bulk delete request.

get.summary: Retrieves all Recon assets for the authenticated customer.

post.summary: Creates a new Recon asset.

parameters.name: startScan
parameters.description: If true, immediately queue a scan for the new asset.

requestBody.description: The asset configuration.

get.summary: Retrieves a specific Recon asset by ID.

requestBody.description: The updated asset configuration.

delete.summary: Deletes a Recon asset and all associated jobs and schedules.

ActionType.description: Possible values: `0` = Create, `1` = Update, `2` = Delete, `3` = InviteUser, `4` = DeleteUser, `5` = UpdateUserProfile, `6` = ResetPassword, `7` = EnrollMfa, `8` = DeleteMfa, `9` = ResendInviteEmail

BulkDeleteScheduleSuccessItem.description: Success item in bulk delete schedule response.

BulkScheduleFailureItem.description: Failure item in bulk schedule response.

BulkScheduleSuccessItem.description: Success item in bulk schedule response.

DayOfWeek.description: Possible values: `0` = Sunday, `1` = Monday, `2` = Tuesday, `3` = Wednesday, `4` = Thursday, `5` = Friday, `6` = Saturday

reportId.description: The ID of the test report this finding belongs to.

vulnerableParameterValue.description: The value of the vulnerable parameter that triggered the finding.

fromUrl.description: The URL from which the request was made.

remediationAdvice.description: Recommended remediation steps for this finding.

FindingDetailDto.description: A specific finding instance tied to a test report.

created.description: When this issue was first discovered.

severity.description: Severity level derived from the CVSS score: Info, Low, Medium, or High.

toUrl.description: The URL where the vulnerability was found.

httpMethod.description: The HTTP method used to trigger the vulnerability.

vulnerableParameter.description: The name of the vulnerable parameter.

resolved.description: Whether this issue has been resolved.

rejected.description: Whether this issue has been rejected (false positive).

accepted.description: Whether this issue has been accepted (risk accepted).

applicationId.description: The ID of the application this issue belongs to.

IssueDetailDto.description: Detailed view of a vulnerability issue, including the most recent finding and vulnerability metadata.

assetIds.description: List of asset IDs to delete schedules from.

ReconAssetBulkDeleteScheduleRequestDto.description: Request DTO for bulk deleting schedules from Recon assets.

succeeded.description: List of assets where schedules were deleted.

failed.description: List of assets where the operation failed.

ReconAssetBulkDeleteScheduleResponseDto.description: Response DTO for bulk delete schedule operations.

assetIds.description: List of asset IDs to apply the schedule to.

ReconAssetBulkScheduleRequestDto.description: Request DTO for bulk schedule operations on Recon assets.

succeeded.description: List of assets where the operation succeeded.

failed.description: List of assets where the operation failed.

ReconAssetBulkScheduleResponseDto.description: Response DTO for bulk schedule operations.

name.description:
The domain or IP address (CIDR notation supported) of the asset.
Required for creation.

ports.description: List of ports to scan. If not provided, defaults to standard ports.

discoverDomains.description: Enable automatic discovery of related domains/IP ranges.

assumeNonPublic.description: Assume the asset is non-public. If services respond, alerts will be created.

assumedHostnames.description: List of expected hostnames for this asset. Only applicable when AssumeNonPublic is true.

autoCreateAppsInScan.description: Automatically create web applications in Scan when services are found.

ReconAssetDto.description: Request DTO for creating or updating a Recon asset.

ReconAssetLatestJobDto.description: Latest job status for an asset.

name.description: The domain or IP address of the asset.

discoverDomains.description: Whether automatic domain discovery is enabled.

assumeNonPublic.description: Whether the asset is assumed non-public.

assumedHostnames.description: List of expected hostnames for this asset. Only populated when AssumeNonPublic is true.

autoCreateAppsInScan.description: Whether apps are automatically created in Scan.

isRoot.description: Whether this asset was added by a user (true) or auto-discovered (false).

ReconAssetResponseDto.description: Response DTO for a Recon asset, including latest job status and schedule summary.

timeOfDay.description: Time of day to run the scan (HH:mm format).

dayOfWeeks.description: Days of the week for weekly schedules.

dayOfMonth.description: Day of the month for monthly schedules (1-31).

ReconAssetScheduleDto.description: Request DTO for creating or updating a schedule for a Recon asset.

assetId.description: Asset ID this schedule belongs to.

hasSchedule.description: Whether the asset has any schedules configured.

ReconJobStatus.description: Possible values: `0` = Queued, `1` = Scanning, `2` = Completed, `3` = Error

ScheduleConflictBehavior.description:
Behavior when setting a schedule on an asset that already has one.

Possible values: `0` = Replace, `1` = Add, `2` = Fail

Severity.description: Possible values: `0` = Info, `1` = Low, `2` = Medium, `3` = High

UserLoginStatus.description: Possible values: `0` = Successful, `1` = LoginFormNotFound, `2` = UsernameInvalid, `3` = PasswordInvalid, `4` = TwoFactorAuthenticationInvalid, `5` = TimedOut, `6` = UnknownError

abbreviation.description: Short abbreviation for the vulnerability type.

cweId.description: CWE (Common Weakness Enumeration) identifier, if available.

owaspTopTen2021.description: OWASP Top Ten 2021 category name, if applicable.

owaspTopTenApi2023.description: OWASP Top Ten API 2023 category name, if applicable.

owaspTopTenLlmV11.description: OWASP Top Ten LLM v1.1 category name, if applicable.

owaspResourceUrl.description: Link to the relevant OWASP resource, if available.

VulnerabilityInfoDto.description: Vulnerability metadata from the known vulnerability dictionary.

ApiToken.description: Enter the token with the `WAS` prefix, e.g. `WAS vPhAHspjbWMXaiyJfAXjFHAEzpRQaYuG`.You can create an API key on the Settings page in the F5 Distributed Cloud Web App Scanning console.
ApiToken.name: Authorization

tags.name: Recon Asset Schedules
tags.description: API endpoints for managing schedules on Recon assets.

tags.name: Recon Assets
tags.description: API endpoints for managing Recon assets.
